methodsAttempted.query: site:abdn.ac.uk University of Aberdeen generative AI artificial intelligence assessment academic integrity students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located official University of Aberdeen StaffNet GenAI assessment guidance, student academic-integrity guidance, and University-hosted GenAI PDFs.

methodsAttempted.query: site:abdn.ac.uk/staffnet/education/generative-ai-in-education University of Aberdeen Generative AI in Education guidance students staff
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located the central University of Aberdeen Generative AI in Education page and a research page that was not used for binding policy claims.
Followed official links from StaffNet and Students pages to GenAI assessment guidance, staff guidance PDF, student guidance PDF, and acknowledgement PDF.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Aberdeen discovery found central GenAI principles, assessment, student academic-integrity, staff guidance, student guidance, and acknowledgement/citation guidance. A SharePoint-linked permitted-levels document was inaccessible without authenticated access, and a current GenAI research page was rejected as research/showcase content rather than policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Aberdeen states that it is committed to inclusive, ethical and trustworthy use of AI.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in Education | StaffNet | The University of Aberdeen

artifacts.claimText: Course Coordinators must provide students clear guidance on what GenAI tool use is permitted for work submitted for assessment.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI and Assessments | StaffNet | The University of Aberdeen

artifacts.claimText: The University treats unattributed or unedited GenAI tool use in work submitted for assessment as a form of academic integrity concern.

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity | Students - The University of Aberdeen

artifacts.claimText: Student guidance says students must not use GenAI to do their critical thinking, analysis, evaluation, synthesis, or to generate essays, poems, or creative works.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Students on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education

artifacts.claimText: Student guidance says learning materials and assessment information must not be entered into GenAI tools without explicit permission from the author, and students should never enter personal and sensitive data into GenAI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Students on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education

artifacts.claimText: Staff guidance says staff should not enter University data, including student work, research data, or lecture materials, into GenAI tools without appropriate permissions.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Staff on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education

artifacts.claimText: When GenAI use is permitted for assessment, University guidance says students must acknowledge which tools they used, when they used them, and how they used generated content.

citation.citationTitle: Quick Guide to Acknowledging and Citing the Use of GenAI

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University is committed to inclusive, ethical and trustworthy use of AI.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Course Coordinators must provide clear guidance to students on what is permitted in relation to the use of GenAI tools for the preparation of work that will be submitted for assessment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: How to Talk to Students about GenAI and Assessments?

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI and Assessments | StaffNet | The University of Aberdeen

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative Artificial Intelligence Unattributed or unedited use of GenAi tool in work submitted for assessment.

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity | Students - The University of Aberdeen

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Students on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Important: You must not use GenAI to do your critical thinking, analysis, evaluation, or synthesis. Neither should you use it to generate essays, poems, or any creative works.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Students on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Students on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Please note that any learning materials (e.g. lectures) and assessment information (e.g. questions, tasks, activities) must not be entered into these tools without the explicit permission of the author (e.g. Course Coordinator, Lecturer). What you can do: Never enter personal and sensitive data into GenAI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Students on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Staff on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You should not be entering any University data, such as student work, research data, lecture materials, without appropriate permissions.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Staff on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools in Education

artifacts.sourceTitle: Quick Guide to Acknowledging and Citing the Use of GenAI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If you are permitted to use GenAI tools for your assessment, you must ensure you acknowledge which tools you have used, when you used them, and how you used the content that they generated.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Quick Guide on Acknowledging and Citing the Use of GenAI

citation.citationTitle: Quick Guide to Acknowledging and Citing the Use of GenAI

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow institutional statement copied from the central GenAI in Education page.
Approved because the StaffNet assessment page uses binding 'must provide clear guidance' wording.
Approved as a narrow academic-integrity classification from the student academic-integrity page.
Approved because the student PDF directly says students must not use GenAI for listed critical/creative work.
Approved because the student PDF directly supports both the explicit-permission and personal/sensitive data handling limits.
Approved because the staff PDF directly says University data should not be entered into GenAI tools without appropriate permissions.
Approved because the acknowledgement PDF directly says permitted GenAI use must identify the tools, timing, and use of generated content.

artifacts.title: University of Aberdeen GenAI Guidance Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source candidates for University of Aberdeen GenAI education, assessment, academic-integrity, staff, student, privacy/data-handling, and acknowledgement guidance. One SharePoint-linked permitted-levels document was blocked by authentication and one research page was rejected as non-policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The SharePoint-linked permitted-levels source was not accessible without authentication and was not used as evidence.
No claim was marked human_reviewed, canonical, or publishable.
